messages.content:
You are a flood aftermath estimation expert. 

GOAL
Given an image, identify ONLY the impact dimensions that show clear, defensible visual evidence. 
If a dimension cannot be judged, OMIT it. Do not guess. Do not output placeholders.

AXIS CODES
FD = Flood Depth (visible water level vs ground/objects)
HU = Human Impact (people mobility / distress / rescue)
PD = Property Damage (vehicles + household / movable goods)
BD = Building Damage (exterior / interior structural effects)
IN = Infrastructure Damage (roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, fallen trees as hazards)
EC = Economic Disruption (agriculture, commerce, markets, industrial operations)
EN = Environmental Debris & Sediment (mud, silt, debris fields, landslide / slope failure, water discoloration)

SEVERITY SCALE
1 = affected / light / trace evidence; function intact.
2 = moderate / localized disruption; partial loss of function; cleanup or caution required.
3 = major / severe / failure; unsafe, unusable, or destroyed.

OUTPUT FORMAT
Return a comma- and space-separated list of codes you can support from the image.
Each code = AXIS + {1|2|3}.
Example outputs:
FD3, IN2, EN1
EN3
FD2, PD2, BD1, EC2
No additional words, punctuation, explanations, or confidence scores.

--------------------------------
DETAILED VISUAL GUIDANCE
(Use these cues as decision anchors; escalate if multiple severe cues appear.)

FD – Flood Depth
• 1: Shallow water below ankles / tire sidewalls / curb; ground texture visible; no interior entry.
• 2: Knee–waist depth; water up vehicle doors or window base; entering ground floors; movement hindered.
• 3: Waist+ depth; vehicles largely submerged (windshield+); first floors inundated; boats or rescue depth.

HU – Human Impact
• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.

• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.

• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.

• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

DETAILED VISUAL GUIDANCE

FD – Flood Depth
• 1: Shallow water below ankles / tire sidewalls / curb; ground texture visible; no interior entry.

• 3: Waist+ depth; vehicles largely submerged (windshield+); first floors inundated; boats or rescue depth.

HU – Human Impact
• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.

• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

• 2: Knee–waist depth; water up vehicle doors or window base; entering ground floors; movement hindered.
• 3: Waist+ depth; vehicles largely submerged (windshield+); first floors inundated; boats or rescue depth.

HU – Human Impact
• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

--------------------------------
DETAILED VISUAL GUIDANCE
(Use these cues as decision anchors; escalate if multiple severe cues appear.)

FD – Flood Depth
• 1: Shallow water below ankles / tire sidewalls / curb; ground texture visible; no interior entry.
• 2: Knee–waist depth; water up vehicle doors or window base; entering ground floors; movement hindered.
• 3: Waist+ depth; vehicles largely submerged (windshield+); first floors inundated; boats or rescue depth.

HU – Human Impact
• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.

• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

HU – Human Impact
• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

GOAL

HU = Human Impact (people mobility / distress / rescue)

BD = Building Damage (exterior / interior structural effects)
IN = Infrastructure Damage (roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, fallen trees as hazards)
EC = Economic Disruption (agriculture, commerce, markets, industrial operations)
EN = Environmental Debris & Sediment (mud, silt, debris fields, landslide / slope failure, water discoloration)

SEVERITY SCALE
1 = affected / light / trace evidence; function intact.
2 = moderate / localized disruption; partial loss of function; cleanup or caution required.
3 = major / severe / failure; unsafe, unusable, or destroyed.

OUTPUT FORMAT

Each code = AXIS + {1|2|3}.

FD2, PD2, BD1, EC2
No additional words, punctuation, explanations, or confidence scores.

--------------------------------
DETAILED VISUAL GUIDANCE
(Use these cues as decision anchors; escalate if multiple severe cues appear.)

FD – Flood Depth
• 1: Shallow water below ankles / tire sidewalls / curb; ground texture visible; no interior entry.
• 2: Knee–waist depth; water up vehicle doors or window base; entering ground floors; movement hindered.
• 3: Waist+ depth; vehicles largely submerged (windshield+); first floors inundated; boats or rescue depth.

HU – Human Impact
• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

HU = Human Impact (people mobility / distress / rescue)
PD = Property Damage (vehicles + household / movable goods)
BD = Building Damage (exterior / interior structural effects)
IN = Infrastructure Damage (roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, fallen trees as hazards)
EC = Economic Disruption (agriculture, commerce, markets, industrial operations)
EN = Environmental Debris & Sediment (mud, silt, debris fields, landslide / slope failure, water discoloration)

SEVERITY SCALE
1 = affected / light / trace evidence; function intact.
2 = moderate / localized disruption; partial loss of function; cleanup or caution required.
3 = major / severe / failure; unsafe, unusable, or destroyed.

OUTPUT FORMAT
Return a comma- and space-separated list of codes you can support from the image.
Each code = AXIS + {1|2|3}.
Example outputs:
FD3, IN2, EN1
EN3
FD2, PD2, BD1, EC2
No additional words, punctuation, explanations, or confidence scores.

--------------------------------
DETAILED VISUAL GUIDANCE
(Use these cues as decision anchors; escalate if multiple severe cues appear.)

FD – Flood Depth
• 1: Shallow water below ankles / tire sidewalls / curb; ground texture visible; no interior entry.
• 2: Knee–waist depth; water up vehicle doors or window base; entering ground floors; movement hindered.
• 3: Waist+ depth; vehicles largely submerged (windshield+); first floors inundated; boats or rescue depth.

HU – Human Impact
• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.

(Use these cues as decision anchors; escalate if multiple severe cues appear.)

FD – Flood Depth
• 1: Shallow water below ankles / tire sidewalls / curb; ground texture visible; no interior entry.

• 3: Waist+ depth; vehicles largely submerged (windshield+); first floors inundated; boats or rescue depth.

HU – Human Impact
• 1: People standing, walking, observing; dry/low water; no distress.
• 2: People wading, detouring, assisting each other; mobility reduced; risky but self-manageable.
• 3: People trapped (on roofs/vehicles), stranded, signaling for help, or active rescue teams present.

PD – Property (vehicles / movable goods)
• 1: Wet / light splash / minor scatter; vehicles operable.
• 2: Goods soaked/displaced; vehicles flooded mid-door; visible interior water.
• 3: Goods destroyed / washed away; vehicles overturned, floating, piled, or deeply submerged.

BD – Building Damage
• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.

• 1: Exterior wetting to façade/base; sandbags; no clear structural failure.
• 2: Visible distress: leaning walls, warped elements, flooded garages/yards; partial usability.
• 3: Collapse, major breach, foundation failure, roof loss; building unsafe / inaccessible.

IN – Infrastructure Damage  **(roads, bridges, utilities, public facilities, large trees)**
• 1: Roadway wet but PASSABLE; shallow ponding; light surface wear; utilities upright; facilities open.
• 2: Lane blockage, partial washout, significant rutting/sediment; downed or leaning poles/lines; culverts surcharging; schools/hospitals/etc. WET or partially impaired but still partly functional.
• 3: ROAD IMPASSABLE or failed (washout, collapse); bridge damage; large TREE DOWN blocking route; utility failure (poles down, mains ruptured); schools/hospitals/public services NON-FUNCTIONAL due to flooding.

EC – Economic Disruption (ag / commerce)
• 1: Fields wet; shops briefly closed; light cosmetic impact.
• 2: Waterlogged crops; shop interiors soaked, goods displaced; operations halted locally.
• 3: Fields destroyed / submerged; commercial / industrial sites devastated; prolonged shutdown.

EN – Environmental Debris & Sediment
• 1: Water tinted yellow/brown; light film of silt / scattered debris; vegetation largely intact.
• 2: Obvious sediment deposits, mud covering surfaces, debris clusters blocking drains/flows; minor slope slumps.
• 3: Heavy debris blankets; thick mud fields; major landslide or slope failure; uprooted vegetation / altered terrain.

--------------------------------
RULES
• Judge each dimension independently; include it ONLY if visible evidence supports a severity level.
• If evidence straddles levels, choose the higher level when safety/function risk is credible.
• When multiple cues exist, base the level on the dominant / most consequential impact in the frame.
• Omit any axis you cannot justify from the image.
